description: 'theali' describes suitability to the present purpose and circumstances. A method may fit one task and fail another because the conditions have changed. 'telua' (accurate) compares a representation with a fact or reference, 'kithela' (valid) concerns an argument's structure, and 'thosami' (compatible) concerns things that can work together.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', let air pass along it, and move into true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Lower the tongue into a separate stressed open central 'a', keeping the 'e.a' hiatus distinct. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', let the voice pass along its sides, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: A fine dental stream opens across the stressed hiatus, then the tongue settles into the lateral contact of 'li'. The light final placement gives the word a bodily image of finding a fit.
usage_notes: Before a verb, the adjective gives fitting manner: 'theali nila' can mean see fittingly or see rightly. The quality noun is fittingness. A nearby phrase can name the purpose or circumstances when context does not make them clear.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A reply can be judged against the present purpose and relationship. 'theali' directs correction towards the response and its context rather than the speaker's worth.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A fitting planting time depends on the crop and season; the calendar alone does not settle it.

semantic_domains.activity: an action or method suited to its present purpose and conditions
semantic_domains.cognition: a contextual judgement of suitability rather than factual accuracy
semantic_domains.wisdom: selection of a response appropriate to the situation and relationship
